---
title: "Luis Mario Ramirez Cruz"
subtitle: "Quantitative Ecology | UC Santa Cruz"
#image: images/couch-kits.JPG
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/Ch1ll1Peppers1
    - icon: bluesky
      text: Bluesky
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/afredston.bsky.social
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=zStqHYEAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:lramir58@ucsc.edu
---
Hey! I'm a quantitative biologist and undergraduate researcher at the University of California Santa Cruz pursuing a **double major in Computer Engineering and Biology**.

My research integrates **data science, ecological modeling, and conservation biology** to understand how environmental change affects species distributions and population dynamics.

I currently work on **species distribution models and dynamic range models of Pacific rockfish** to improve forecasts of marine populations under climate change.

## Research Interests

- Species Distribution Modeling (SDMs)
- Population and Range Dynamics
- Climate Change Ecology
- Marine and Alpine Ecosystems
- Computational Ecology
- Geospatial Analysis

## Current Affiliation

University of California Santa Cruz  
Computational Biology & Ecology Researcher

## Current Research

**The Fredston Lab — UC Santa Cruz**

Comparing the performance of statistical **Species Distribution Models** and mechanistic **Dynamic Range Models** to forecast the distribution of **Shortbelly Rockfish** in California.

This work examines how recruitment variability, spatial population dynamics, and environmental drivers affect abundance in a commercially important groundfish species.

## Contact

Email: lramir58@ucsc.edu
